summary: Vivid audience tape of the 04‑15‑83 show captures a high‑energy performance (Shakedown, Brown‑Eyed Women, GDTRFB) with good balance and only a brief glitch.
review: The recording is an audience capture using a Sennheiser mic that delivers a lively, well‑balanced sound; the only technical flaw is a short cut between GDTRFB and Around & Around. Musically the show is celebrated by reviewers for its hot second set, standout jams on Shakedown Street, Feel Like A Stranger, Bob Star, GDTRFB and the seamless flow into Around & Around, with Jerry Garcia repeatedly praised as the driving force.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are detailed and overwhelmingly positive about both performance and tape quality; only a minor recording glitch is noted, so the combined rating is strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced Sennheiser audience mic capture with a lively atmosphere. One reviewer notes a brief cut between GDTRFB and Around & Around but otherwise no major issues.

standout_songs: It's All Over Now, Baby Blue
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe a strong first set with classics like Shakedown Street and a second set that ‘went into hyper‑drive’, noting tight transitions, extended jams and an especially magical Bob Star moment that propelled the show to its peak.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised – “Jerry rocks GDTRFB”, “Jerry was the driver of the bus”, noted for his jam on Stones and overall guitar fire.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for singing the rare Bob Star transition and leading vocals on several numbers.
